book_claims.claim: Used in the Longevity Diet barley salad and as a dessert nut; provides omega-3 ALA (0.25–0.29 g per ¼ cup)
book_claims.mechanism: Source of plant omega-3 ALA and healthy unsaturated fats

supplementary_research.finding: Pecan consumption associated with reduced LDL oxidation and total cholesterol in controlled trial; antioxidant capacity among highest of tree nuts
Tree nut consumption (including pecans) associated with 19% reduced cardiovascular mortality and 11% reduced all-cause mortality in meta-analysis

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Plant-form omega-3; ~5–15% conversion to EPA/DHA; contributes to omega-3 targets
Primary fatty acid; cardioprotective; lowers LDL when replacing saturated fat
Gamma-tocopherol form predominant; synergistic with other antioxidants

synergies.description: Longevity Diet barley salad pairing; nut fat improves absorption of fat-soluble vitamins in grain dishes
Both tree nuts recommended in Longevity Diet; walnuts have higher ALA, pecans higher antioxidant content
Traditional dessert pairing; nut fat slows sugar absorption from fruit, reducing glycemic spike
